headline: A breach of the Klue Battlecards app handed the Icarus extortion crew Salesforce data from security firms Huntress and Recorded Future, extending a run of SaaS supply-chain compromises.

emerging_trends.text: Microsoft's AutoJack chain shows a single malicious webpage can hijack an AI browsing agent into running code on its host, the latest proof that autonomous agents and the orphaned ones left behind widen the enterprise attack surface.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AutoJack Turns AI Browsing Agents Into RCE

emerging_trends.text: Stolen OAuth tokens from the Klue Battlecards app let the Icarus crew loot Salesforce records from Huntress and Recorded Future, the third trusted integration attackers have abused as a back door into customers' CRM data.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue OAuth Breach Feeds Salesforce Data Theft

emerging_trends.text: Attackers reached the Splunk Enterprise remote-code-execution flaw within days of disclosure, forcing a three-day CISA patch deadline as F5 rushed out-of-band fixes for two critical NGINX bugs.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Splunk and NGINX Flaws Draw Urgent Patches

topics.title: Klue OAuth Breach Feeds Salesforce Data Theft
topics.latest_developments: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration after the Icarus threat actors abused OAuth tokens from a June 11 incident to steal CRM data from customers including Huntress and Recorded Future.
topics.summary: Klue, a competitive-intelligence platform, marks the third integrated Salesforce app attackers have compromised to siphon customer data in an ongoing extortion campaign. Organizations should audit connected-app permissions and rotate OAuth tokens.

sources.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise

topics.title: Splunk and NGINX Flaws Draw Urgent Patches
topics.latest_developments: CISA added Splunk Enterprise flaw CVE-2026-20253, an unauthenticated remote-code-execution bug, to its known exploited catalog and gave agencies three days to patch, while F5 shipped out-of-band fixes for two critical NGINX Open Source flaws led by CVE-2026-42530, a use-after-free in the ngx_http_v3_module.
topics.summary: Attackers exploited the Splunk missing-authentication flaw within days of its disclosure; the NGINX bugs let a remote unauthenticated attacker run code on HTTP/3-enabled servers. Splunk separately patched an OS command injection in its AI Toolkit, and Atlassian fixed dozens of dependency flaws.

sources.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities

topics.title: AutoJack Turns AI Browsing Agents Into RCE
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft detailed AutoJack, an exploit chain in which a single malicious webpage drives an AI browsing agent into remote code execution on its host by abusing localhost trust, missing authentication, and unsafe parameter handling in AutoGen Studio's MCP WebSocket.
topics.summary: The research shows that when agents browse untrusted content and reach local services, traditional boundaries collapse. The risk compounds with orphaned agents left running after their creators leave, which carry standing privileges no one tracks.

sources.title: AutoJack: How a single page can RCE the host running your AI agent
Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network

topics.title: INC Ransomware Tops 830 Victims
topics.latest_developments: Acronis charted INC's rise to at least 830 victims since August 2023, crediting affiliates who migrated after the disruption of LockBit and the shutdown of BlackCat.
topics.summary: INC operates as a ransomware-as-a-service business that has become one of 2026's most prolific extortion groups, favoring healthcare and other sectors where disruption forces a fast payout. Defenders should harden remote access and segment recovery backups.

sources.title: INC Ransomware Emerges as Major RaaS Threat in 2026 with 830+ Victims Since 2023

topics.title: Popa Botnet Tied to NASDAQ-Listed Alarum
topics.latest_developments: Researchers from multiple firms concluded that the four-year-old Popa botnet, which forces millions of consumer Android TV boxes to relay traffic for advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass scraping, links to NetNut, a residential-proxy provider run by publicly traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd.
topics.summary: Popa converts compromised TV boxes into exit nodes for paying proxy customers, hiding malicious traffic behind ordinary home IP addresses. The finding ties a commercial residential-proxy business to a long-running consumer-device botnet.

sources.title: 'Popa' Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm

topics.title: China's UNC6508 Hunts Outdated REDCap Servers
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek reported that most internet-accessible REDCap research-data servers run outdated software, and that China-linked UNC6508 regularly targets them for initial access and backdoor deployment.
topics.summary: REDCap is a widely used clinical and research data-capture platform run by universities and medical institutions. Operators should update to current releases and pull exposed instances behind authentication to deny UNC6508 a foothold.

sources.title: Majority of Internet-Accessible REDCap Servers Outdated

weather: Juneteenth: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Rain Showers, high 78F.
Saturday: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 78F.

business_politics.title: Burnham Wins By-Election, Eyes Starmer's Job
business_politics.latest_developments: Andy Burnham won the Makerfield by-election on June 19, taking a Commons seat that lets him challenge Keir Starmer for the Labour leadership, and Starmer said he would stand in any contest; ten-year gilt yields rose 6.5 basis points to 4.809%.
business_politics.summary: Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham defeated Nigel Farage's Reform UK in the Makerfield special election, returning to Parliament with a platform to mount a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer, a fight that would reshape British government as May public-sector borrowing hit £23.3 billion.

sources.title: Rebel Lawmaker’s Election Win Clears Path to Oust U.K.’s Starmer
Burnham storms to by-election victory in challenge to Starmer
Andy Burnham is now Britain’s prime-minister-in-waiting

business_politics.title: Iran Talks Stall, Vance Cancels Switzerland Trip
business_politics.latest_developments: Vice President JD Vance called off his trip to Switzerland and Iran postponed the nuclear negotiations after Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, casting doubt on Wednesday's interim accord and pushing Brent crude back above $80 a barrel.
business_politics.summary: The United States and Iran signed an interim deal on June 17 to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and the U.S. Navy has since let more than a dozen ships reach Iranian ports, while the Pentagon told lawmakers it needs $80 billion to cover the war's costs.

sources.title: Iran postponed US talks due to Israeli strikes on Lebanon, diplomats say
Oil Rises After Vance Postpones Travel to Switzerland For Iran Talks
Pentagon Tells Lawmakers It Needs $80 Billion for Iran War and Other Bills

business.title: PRT Keeps Bus Service at the Waterfront
business.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Regional Transit starts serving two new stops at the Waterfront shopping center in Homestead on Sunday, the result of a fall agreement that reversed the center's move to push buses off the property near Target and Giant Eagle.
business.summary: Pittsburgh Regional Transit and the Waterfront's management settled a fight over bus access to the Homestead retail complex, and the agency will begin using two relocated stops this weekend, preserving service for the shoppers and workers who reach the stores by transit.

sources.title: PRT to begin serving new bus stops at The Waterfront this weekend

business.title: Nine-Story Tower Proposed on Mt. Washington
business.latest_developments: WPXI reports some neighbors now oppose the nine-story residential building a developer wants to raise at 301 Grandview Avenue, a project that still requires demolishing the church on the lot.
business.summary: A developer has proposed a nine-story apartment building at 301 Grandview Avenue atop Mt. Washington, a high-profile site along the Grandview promenade overlooking downtown Pittsburgh, and the plan would tear down the church that occupies the lot.

sources.title: 9-story high-rise planned on site of Mt. Washington church, though some neighbors not sold

around_town.title: City Adds $800,000 for Homeless Street Outreach
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh is directing $800,000 to its Roots street outreach team to expand support for homeless residents, though advocates told the Post-Gazette that significant gaps in services remain.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh's Roots street outreach team, which works directly with people living unsheltered across the city, will receive $800,000 in new funding, money advocates welcome even as they warn it falls short of the need.

sources.title: Pittsburgh’s street outreach team is getting $800K to help support homeless people. Advocates say plenty of gaps remain.

around_town.title: Pittsburgh Expands Dolly Parton's Imagination Library
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh first lady Katie O'Connor announced Thursday in Carrick that the city, backed by Benter Foundation funding, is widening its rollout of Dolly Parton's Imagination Library to reach more children.
around_town.summary: Dolly Parton's Imagination Library mails free books each month to enrolled children, and Pittsburgh is enrolling more families citywide with Benter Foundation support to lift early literacy.

sources.title: Pittsburgh expands efforts to get free books to kids through Dolly Parton's Imagination Library

around_town.title: Rodef Shalom and Temple Sinai Merge as Beit Kulanu
around_town.latest_developments: In a Post-Gazette conversation, Rabbi Daniel Fellman described the merger of Pittsburgh's Rodef Shalom and Temple Sinai congregations into a single Reform community, Beit Kulanu, which he calls 'a house for all peoples.'
around_town.summary: Two longtime Pittsburgh Reform Jewish congregations, Rodef Shalom and Temple Sinai, are combining into one community named Beit Kulanu under Rabbi Daniel Fellman, consolidating membership and worship under a single roof.

sources.title: Brandon McGinley: 'A house for all peoples': A conversation with Rabbi Daniel Fellman

events.title: Western Pa. Juneteenth and Black Music Celebration
events.latest_developments: The four-day festival continues through Sunday, June 21, with the Juneteenth holiday itself falling on Friday, June 19.
events.summary: The Western Pennsylvania Juneteenth and Black Music Celebration, the largest Juneteenth festival in North America, runs Thursday through Sunday, June 18 to 21, from 11 a.m. to 10 p.m. across Point State Park, Market Square, and Liberty Avenue downtown; Stop the Violence Pittsburgh presents the 161st-anniversary event.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from Juneteenth and Monster Jam to Summer Splash and Bubblesburgh

events.title: Plum Summerfest at Larry Mills Park
events.latest_developments: Plum's Summerfest opens this weekend at Larry Mills Park with a Ferris wheel, fishing games, funnel cakes, and corn dogs.
events.summary: Summerfest, Plum's community fair, sets up at Larry Mills Park this weekend with carnival rides including a Ferris wheel, fishing games, and fair food such as funnel cakes and corn dogs.

sources.title: Summerfest event kicks off at Larry Mills Park in Plum

around_teams.title: Pirates Trade Joey Bart to Braves for Hunter Stratton
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pirates sent catcher Joey Bart to the Atlanta Braves on Thursday night for right-handed reliever Hunter Stratton, a move the Post-Gazette frames as adding bullpen depth and clearing a logjam behind the plate.
around_teams.summary: Hunter Stratton, a 2017 Pirates draft pick who pitched in Pittsburgh from 2023 to 2025 before Atlanta acquired him last summer, returns to the organization and reports to Triple-A Indianapolis, while Bart's exit thins a crowded Pirates catching picture.

sources.title: The Pirates trade Joey Bart, adding bullpen help and solving their logjam at catcher

around_teams.title: Steelers Expected to Pass on Brendan Sorsby
around_teams.latest_developments: Post-Gazette writers say the Steelers will likely avoid quarterback Brendan Sorsby in the NFL's supplemental draft, pointing to the gambling matter that pushed him from the college ranks.
around_teams.summary: Brendan Sorsby, a college quarterback who entered the NFL supplemental draft amid a gambling matter, has surfaced as a possible Steelers target; the team's beat writers expect Pittsburgh to steer clear, citing the risk and a settled quarterback room.

sources.title: Don’t expect the Steelers to make a bid for Brendan Sorsby in the NFL’s supplemental draft

around_teams.title: Rookie Safety Spears-Jennings's 4.32 Forty
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature details how rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings ran a 4.32-second 40-yard dash at the NFL combine and turned the time into a Steelers selection.
around_teams.summary: Robert Spears-Jennings, a safety the Steelers drafted, traces his rise to a 4.32-second 40-yard dash at the scouting combine, a number that vaulted him up draft boards and into Pittsburgh's plans.

sources.title: How Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings changed his NFL trajectory in 4.32 seconds

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson talks with Michael Morton about how AI reshapes e-commerce, weighing unfalsifiable bear cases, the difference between distribution and referral models, and AI's reach into grocery and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron publishes financials showing OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending reached $34 billion, arguing the company burns cash at a pace that leaves it with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn’t Breaking Work. It’s Already Broken.
reading.summary: Reacting to a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds about a 6,000-person survey, Newport argues knowledge work already buckled under overload and busywork, so AI lands on a system that broke long before it arrived.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
